title: Three Kinds of Renunciation | Bhagavad Gita 18.01 - 18.03 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai.
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, we'll start the discussion on the 18th chapter, text number 4 to 8, in fact 9 also,
and the topic is three kinds of renunciation. In these verses, Krishna is talking about
renunciation in goodness, renunciation in passion and renunciation in ignorance.
How many of us were there last to last class when we talked about renunciation,
where a person is walking down the street, he finds a wallet? How many of us were there?
Before we get into the discussion, what is renunciation? Anyone can help me with an
answer. What is renunciation? What is true renunciation? Surrender to God. Let's think
about it. What is true renunciation? In that topic, in that discussion, we talked about
what is false renunciation, falgu vairagya, and what is real renunciation. It is not,
if a person just gives up his wealth, family, house, no, no, it's all material, it's all material, that necessarily is not renunciation.
What is real renunciation? So, just to repeat, some of you are not there. A person walking down the road,
he sees a wallet full of 2000 rupee notes. He was walking in the US dollars.
And predominantly, there are three ways in which a person, three options a person has.
Let's go by option number one. What is option one?
With notes coming out of that wallet, very tempting. So, a person is walking down, he sees the wallet, he's having serious financial challenges also.
Option one, what does he say? He says, it's not mine. Why I should take? It's not mine. And he just walks away. It's tempting, but he says, it's not mine. He leaves it and walks away.
He doesn't get tempted.
So, that is one kind of renunciation.
You agreed, everyone?
Another person or the same person, another scenario or another option is that he sees this wallet.
He sees left, he sees right, he sees in the front, he sees in the backside, he sees up, he sees down.
Make sure that nobody is watching. What he does?
Takes the wallet, puts in his pocket.
That's second way of acting in this scenario.
Is that okay?
That is second type.
Third type, third option, third way of acting when you see a wallet.
What is that?
You pick it up, understand it is not mine.
Understand that it belongs to someone.
And go and get it.
And give back to him.
Again, it is not mine.
Wallet is in your hand.
Nobody is watching.
You realize it is not mine.
You don't enjoy it, knowing that it is not mine.
Still, it belongs to someone.
Find out to whom it belongs to and go and give it back to him.
Of the three options, which one is best?
Everyone agrees?
Third one.
Real renunciation is acting basis option number three.
You have wallet in your hand.
You are amidst lot of opulence.
Lord has blessed you with prosperity.
You realize it is not mine.
It belongs to Lord.
And you use it in the service of the Lord, glorification of the Lord.
To offer it back to the Lord with gratitude.
That is real renunciation.
And that real renunciation has to be cultivated by artificially abnegating, giving up our material possessions.
The person who abnegates will not necessarily bring about renunciation.
For example, the person is saying it is not mine and just gives up.
Will not necessarily bring about renunciation.
That purification means we get purified of our mentality of Aham Mameti.
I and mine.
It is mine.
It is meant for my enjoyment.
That's why Krishna in many many places in the Gita says, do not be attached to the results.
One who enjoys the result of his activity is a miser.
He will get implicated, he will get entangled.
Yagnad karmano anyatra loko yam karma bandhana.
Any activity we do which is not done as a yagya, yagya means for the satisfaction of
the Supreme, loko yam karma bandhana, that activity binds us.
So we will start the discussion.
Text 4, based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada.
O best of Bharatas, hear from me now about renunciation.
O tiger of among men!
There are three kinds of renunciation declared in scriptures.
Now as I mentioned earlier, different people have different opinions about what is renunciation.
There are people practicing renunciation thinking that this is renunciation.
If you go to Kumbh Mela, there are Babas, body covered with ashes.
The spirit of renunciation is there.
Some people are meditating in Himalayas.
Some people have given up their families.
They have taken some houses in some holy places.
Some people go on pilgrimages, extended period of time, according to them that is renunciation.
So different people have different opinions about renunciation.
Now here, we are going to hear the opinion of, opinion of Lord Krishna Himself.
Now hear from me Krishna is saying, hear from me.
Here the Lord is personally present.
So this is, if Bhagavad Gita is read and understood in the spirit that these are the words spoken
by the Supreme Personality of Godhead who is above illusion.
We all, no matter how great a person be.
We all are subject to four divisions.
We all are subject to four divisions.
defects we inherently have four defects can anyone tell me what are the four defects
go one by one we all have imperfect senses
to give an example of imperfect senses
sun is 14 000 times bigger than earth but
seeing with your eyes you cannot figure out today because we have studied it in science we have
faith in science we believe when the sun is rising on the horizon it looks like a small disk of fire
but it is 14 000 times bigger than earth we believe that how many of us believe that sun
is 14 000 times bigger than earth but our senses
give us some other information so our senses are limited second
we have tendency to cheat this is the imperfection inbuilt in a conditioned soul
third we are subject to illusion and fourth we commit mistakes so that way any man-made
knowledge or knowledge given by any great man even though the intention is not to cheat
intention is not to mislead inherently these four defects creep into that understanding
i have given this example let's say a person who is a merchant he is weighing

if there is inherent defect in the bane scale or in the weights
actually this is 990 grammes
even though his intention is correct even though he doesn't want to cheat he
ends up cheating the customers so that's why if we read gita
in the spirit that these are the words spoken by the lord who is
effect, who is above these four defects. That's why words of Acharyas have to be
heard submissively because they are above these four defects. So here the
Lord is personally present and he is saying, now hear from me what is real
renunciation. What is renunciation in three types. Text 5, acts of sacrifice
charity and penance are not to be given up but should be performed . So, sometimes
people might think this charity , sacrifice , penance, why we should do all
those things? All those things should be renounced, should be given up. So Krishna
saying, should not be given up but should be performed. Indeed, charity, sacrifice and
penance purify even great souls. Because these acts, what does these acts do? Penance, sacrifice
and what does it do? It purifies us, frees us from, frees us from? Maya, illusion, frees
us from I and mine. That understanding that this is mine, it is meant for my enjoyment,
acts of sacrifice, acts of penance, austerities, frees us from that understanding. So Krishna
is saying, it should never be given up.
Thinking that these acts are material. And let me renounce all these acts. There are
many purificatory processes for advancing in human life. So human form of life, all
the scriptures, different rituals, different sacrifices, different yagyas, different penances,
different austerities, different vratas. Like Ekadashi Vrata. It's a vrata. There are many
sacrifices, many rituals.
And the objective of all these rituals is purify. Is purify us. Purify our consciousness.
Elevate our consciousness. Unfortunately, many of these sacrifices, many of the rituals,
we follow but without understanding the spirit behind those rituals or those sacrifices.
Why we are doing?
In India, we observe many festivals. Recently, Holi was there, Lodi was there, all of these festivals, if you see, Holi is going to come soon.
All these festivals have spiritual significance. But today's world, not many people know about that.
We end up just doing it because it's part of our family culture or tradition or festivals which we have to observe.
So it's meant for purification and so that a person advances in his spiritual understanding, comes closer to God.
Just like, for example, Yagya, this Vivaha Yagya. What is Vivaha? Marriage. It's a Yagya.
Our senses are going berserk, uncontrolled, untamed. In human form of life, our scriptures, all scriptures for that matter,
have a concept of marriage. Animals don't have that. Why? To regulate our tendencies, our urges.
Because unless we are regulated, unless we are controlled, unless we tame our senses, higher understanding is,
we are disqualified for higher understanding.
So that's why these sacrifices are there.
These sacrifices, these Yagyas, these rituals are part of our culture.
All prescribed sacrifices are meant for achieving the Supreme Lord.
It may look little far-fetched, indirect, but all these sacrifices which are authorized,
which are mentioned in the scriptures, all the vratas, even Ekadashi.
Why do we follow Ekadashi? What is the spirit behind all fasting?
Can anyone tell me? Why are we fasting?
A lot of people do vrata or fasting. It's good for my health. My digestive system gets rest.
Why do we follow Ekadashi vrata?
Ekadashi is one example. There are a lot of fasting days, right?
Half-day fasting, fasting till dusk. Why fasting?
How the Lord is pleased. Did the Lord give food?
Why on festival days?
Why on festival days?
The spirit behind all such vratas is, we minimize our bodily,
fulfilling bodily demands and focus on fulfilling the demands of soul.
Generally, we are so absorbed fulfilling the demands of the body,
fulfilling the needs of the soul takes a back seat.
See, all of us are so busy, 24 hours we have,
but you know, even to find five, to chant four rounds, two rounds,
it's a struggle for many of us.
What is the balance 24 hours we are engaged in? Fulfilling the bodily demands.
Fulfilling the demands of something which is connected to the body,
my near and dear ones,
my friends, family, all connected to the body.
So, the spirit behind these vratas, the sacrifices is
to fulfill the requirements of the soul.
Requirement of the soul whereby it comes closer to God.
Because that soul is part and parcel of God.
Similarly, charity is for purification of heart.
How does charity purifies one's heart? Charity.
Giving something which belongs to us, to the Lord, to someone else,
how does it purify? Reduces attachment, yes.
And Krishna also in the Gita says there are three kinds of charity.
Charity in goodness, charity in passion, charity in ignorance.
So charity, when we give in the spirit of that this belongs to the Lord,
has to be used in the service of the Lord, that charity purifies us.
Brings us closer to God, pleases the God, pleases Lord.
Text 6.
All these activities should be performed without any expectation of results.
Many times these acts for purification,
which are originally meant for purification,
originally meant us to bring us closer to God,
we end up doing those activities for some material gains.
Charity for material gains, sacrifices for material gains,
rituals for material gains.

Has to be undertaken, has to be, we have to, we have to pursue those sacrifices.
We should follow those things.
Sacrifices which are meant for material gains should be abnegated, should be given up, should be renounced.
A devotee of the Lord should accept any kind of work, sacrifice or charity that will help him elevate in his consciousness, purify his existence.
